summary: A compact two-story residential structure with a stepped pyramidal roof. The building features stonebrick walls with alternating wood plank trim bands, glass windows on both floors, and sits on a concrete foundation platform. The roof is constructed from wood planks in a tiered gable configuration.

shape.overall_form: rectangular box with stepped pyramidal roof
shape.symmetry: bilateral symmetry on all visible sides

elements: windows (4 per floor, arranged in 2x2 grid)
stepped pyramidal roof with wood planks
horizontal wood trim bands between floors
rebuild_hints: Build 8x8 base foundation with concrete blocks
Construct first floor walls with stonebrick, 4 blocks high
Add horizontal wood plank trim band at mid-height
Place 4 glass windows (2x2 arrangement) on first floor
Build second floor walls with stonebrick, 4 blocks high
Add second horizontal wood plank trim band
Place 4 glass windows (2x2 arrangement) on second floor
Create stepped pyramidal roof: 8x8 base, then 6x6, then 4x4, then 2x2 using wood planks
Ensure all roof steps are centered and symmetrical
Add concrete platform foundation extending 1-2 blocks beyond building footprint
uncertainties: Exact window frame material (appears to be concrete or stone but not fully clear)
Interior layout and room configuration not visible
Presence of doors or entrance points not visible from exterior views
Exact height measurement uncertain; estimate based on visible block proportions
Roof material texture suggests wood planks but could be alternative wood variant
